name: peak_pricing
description: a time-of-use peak-pricing demand-shifting world; actions consume, shift, store

card.description: a time-of-use peak-pricing demand-shifting world; actions consume, shift, store

rules: Each hour carries a time-of-use price from the 24-hour schedule; an hour is 'peak' when its price >= peak_threshold.
'consume' serves the waiting demand now: it drains the battery first (free at the margin), then buys any remainder from the grid at the current hour's price, and adds it all to 'served'.
'shift' defers the waiting demand to a later hour (adds to 'shifted'); nothing is bought and the demand carries forward.
'store' charges the battery from the grid at the current price, up to charge_rate and battery_cap; it banks energy but serves no demand.
Every action advances the clock one hour, after which a fresh hourly_load block is added to demand. Unknown actions are no-ops and do not advance the clock.
Conservation: the battery is only filled by 'store' and only drained by 'consume', so stored == from_battery + battery at all times.

transition.code:
def transition(state, action):
    s = dict(state)
    prices = [float(p) for p in (s.get("prices") or [])]
    span = len(prices) if prices else 24
    hour = int(s.get("hour", 0)) % span
    thr = float(s.get("peak_threshold", 0.0))

    # current hour's price (derive defensively, e.g. right after a fresh paste)
    price = prices[hour] if prices else float(s.get("price", 0.0))
    s["price"] = round(price, 6)
    s["peak"] = bool(thr > 0 and price >= thr)

    name = action.get("name")
    demand = float(s.get("demand", 0.0))
    battery = float(s.get("battery", 0.0))
    cap = float(s.get("battery_cap", 0.0))
    rate = float(s.get("charge_rate", 0.0))

    if name == "consume":
        from_batt = min(battery, demand)
        battery -= from_batt
        from_grid = demand - from_batt
        s["from_battery"] = round(float(s.get("from_battery", 0.0)) + from_batt, 6)
        s["grid_kwh"] = round(float(s.get("grid_kwh", 0.0)) + from_grid, 6)
        s["cost"] = round(float(s.get("cost", 0.0)) + from_grid * price, 6)
        s["served"] = round(float(s.get("served", 0.0)) + demand, 6)
        demand = 0.0
    elif name == "shift":
        s["shifted"] = round(float(s.get("shifted", 0.0)) + demand, 6)
        # demand carries forward, unserved
    elif name == "store":
        amt = min(rate, max(0.0, cap - battery))      # never overcharge
        battery += amt
        s["stored"] = round(float(s.get("stored", 0.0)) + amt, 6)
        s["grid_kwh"] = round(float(s.get("grid_kwh", 0.0)) + amt, 6)
        s["cost"] = round(float(s.get("cost", 0.0)) + amt * price, 6)
        # demand carries forward, unserved
    else:
        s["battery"] = round(battery, 6)
        return s                                       # unknown action: no-op

    # advance one hour; a fresh block of flexible load arrives
    hour = (hour + 1) % span
    demand = round(demand + float(s.get("hourly_load", 0.0)), 6)
    new_price = prices[hour] if prices else price
    s["hour"] = hour
    s["demand"] = demand
    s["battery"] = round(battery, 6)
    s["price"] = round(new_price, 6)
    s["peak"] = bool(thr > 0 and new_price >= thr)
    return s

emit.report: h{hour} ${price}/kWh peak={peak} | demand {demand} kWh, battery {battery} kWh | served {served}, cost ${cost} (grid {grid_kwh} kWh, shifted {shifted}, stored {stored})
